Title: Use of the growing environment as a source of variation to identify the quantitative trait transcripts and modules of co-expressed genes that determine chlorogenic acid accumulation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major seed storage phenolic contributing to chlorogenic acid accumulation and coffee quality.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Temperature responsive in early endosperm development; high temperature at stage 3 induced expression, whereas cool climates delayed activation with higher expression at stage 4.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Early developmental stages (ST3–ST4); highly connected in late-stage co-expression network.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural temperature differences among 16 field environments
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Temperature-dependent PAL2 expression is associated with timing of CGA biosynthesis; delayed activation under cool climates parallels delayed 5-CQA accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR across environments; HPLC metabolite profiling; temperature–transcript and transcript–metabolite timing analysis; co-expression network analysis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted RT-PCR transcript profiling; HPLC metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL2 transcript accumulation was strongly modulated by temperature during early seed development. 2. Low temperature delayed PAL2 activation and coincided with delayed 5-CQA biosynthesis. 3. PAL2 is a core component of the early phenylpropanoid co-expression module and one of the main hubs in mature seeds.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for climate-responsive CGA accumulation, but not identified here as a QTT for final 5-CQA content.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAL2 is central to the first temperature-sensitive control box, but the paper explicitly concludes that HQT and 4CL8, rather than PAL alone, are the major QTTs for 5-CQA in coffee seed.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major seed storage phenolic contributing to chlorogenic acid accumulation and coffee quality.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Temperature responsive in early endosperm development; high temperature promoted earlier expression at stage 3, whereas cool climates delayed activation and increased stage-4 expression.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Early developmental stages (ST3–ST4); also highly connected in late-stage network.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural temperature differences among 16 field environments
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Temperature-dependent C4H expression is associated with the timing of core phenylpropanoid activation and delayed CGA biosynthesis under cool climates.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR across environments; HPLC metabolite profiling; temperature–transcript timing analysis; co-expression network analysis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted RT-PCR transcript profiling; HPLC metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C4H expression responded strongly to mean temperature during early seed development. 2. Delayed C4H activation under cool climates matched delayed 5-CQA accumulation. 3. C4H forms part of the early CGA biosynthetic co-expression module and a major hub in mature seeds.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker for climate adaptation of phenylpropanoid timing, though not identified as a direct QTT for final chlorogenic acid level in this paper.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Together with PAL2, C4H defines the first temperature-sensitive control box controlling timing rather than final quantity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a hydroxycinnamoyl group from hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA to quinate to form chlorogenic acid, predominantly 5-CQA.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major chlorogenic acid storage form in developing coffee seed; contributes to seed phenolic composition and coffee quality.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during early endosperm development; transcript level correlated with CGA accumulation at stage 4.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest during early seed/endosperm development (stages 3–5), especially around stage 4.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field environments differing mainly in mean temperature during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher early HQT transcript abundance is associated with increased final 5-CQA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR across 16 natural environments; HPLC metabolite profiling; transcript–metabolite correlation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a quantitative trait transcript (QTT) and candidate flux-control step for 5-CQA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted RT-PCR transcript profiling; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HQT transcript abundance at early developmental stages positively correlated with 5-CQA content. 2. HQT was identified as a QTT for chlorogenic acid accumulation in coffee seed. 3. HQT likely contributes to transcriptional control of CGA biosynthetic flux.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for selecting or engineering coffee genotypes with elevated chlorogenic acid content; may be combined with upstream phenylpropanoid genes such as 4CL8.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study treats HQT as one of the strongest core biosynthetic candidates controlling 5-CQA accumulation under environmental variation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core phenylpropanoid pathway supplying activated hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA precursors
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: hydroxycinnamic acids such as coumarate/caffeate/ferulate; CoA; ATP
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids to their CoA thioesters, supplying precursors for CGA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major chlorogenic acid storage form in developing coffee seed; contributes to seed phenolic composition and coffee quality.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Activated very early in seed development as part of an upstream phenylpropanoid module; correlated with 5-CQA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High at stage 3/4 in developing endosperm; part of the early co-expression module.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher early 4CL8 expression is associated with increased final 5-CQA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR across environments; HPLC-based CGA profiling; transcript–metabolite correlation analysis; co-expression network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a QTT and candidate flux-control step for 5-CQA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted RT-PCR transcript profiling; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL8 expression at stage 4 positively correlated with final 5-CQA content. 2. 4CL8 was identified as a QTT for chlorogenic acid accumulation. 3. 4CL8 belongs to an early co-expressed module driving CGA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to increase CGA by enhancing precursor activation and combining with HQT.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among multiple phenylpropanoid genes tested, 4CL8 specifically showed strong positive association with 5-CQA.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid/lignin-related remobilization of chlorogenic acid-derived intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Monolignol biosynthesis branch linked to CGA remobilization
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ferulate-derived phenylpropanoid intermediates in lignin biosynthesis, associated here with remobilization of CGA-related pools toward lignification.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Early F5H1 expression negatively correlated with intermediate 5-FQA and 5-CQA levels.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected during early to mid seed development; implicated in control box 3 linked to remobilization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher early F5H1 expression is associated with lower 5-CQA and 5-FQA accumulation, indicating enhanced remobilization away from CGA pools.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR; HPLC metabolite profiling; transcript–metabolite correlation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a QTT for downstream remobilization rather than direct biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted RT-PCR transcript profiling; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Early F5H1 transcript abundance was negatively correlated with intermediate 5-CQA and 5-FQA amounts. 2. F5H1 was identified as a QTT associated with CGA remobilization toward lignin-related metabolism. 3. F5H1 represents a downstream control point affecting storage phenolic accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for reducing CGA remobilization and preserving higher seed CGA, though manipulation may affect lignin composition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The effect is indirect and interpreted as diversion/remobilization of phenylpropanoid flux toward lignin biosynthesis rather than direct FQA formation.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid/lignin-related remobilization of chlorogenic acid-derived intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes monolignols during lignin polymerization and is associated here with remobilization of CGA-derived phenylpropanoids away from storage pools.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression at stages 3 and 5 negatively correlated with 5-FQA at stage 5 and final 5-CQA content.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Triggered around stage 5 as part of a remobilization/lignification module.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher POD expression is associated with reduced 5-CQA and 5-FQA accumulation, consistent with enhanced remobilization toward lignification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR; HPLC metabolite profiling; transcript–metabolite correlation analysis; co-expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a QTT for downstream remobilization of CGA pools
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted RT-PCR transcript profiling; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. POD transcript abundance negatively correlated with 5-FQA and final 5-CQA levels. 2. POD was identified as a downstream QTT associated with CGA remobilization. 3. POD belongs to a mid-development co-expression module linked to lignin biosynthesis/cell wall thickening.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing POD-mediated remobilization could help retain CGA in seeds, but may compromise lignification and seed structural development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper interprets POD as part of a control box for phenylpropanoid remobilization rather than primary CGA synthesis.
